notes: Migrated from the Aesop repo (ladder2 branch) on 2026-06-10. theladder/ladder2.html and its runtime dependencies are live. The pages below were brought over ONLY as skeleton placeholders (for nav/link continuity) and must be rebuilt for The Ladder AI — do not migrate the heavy legacy versions from Aesop's ai-academy/.

tasks.title: Rebuild the Student Hub
tasks.detail: Rebuild the learner Student Hub for The Ladder AI: certifications earned, tier progress, active/begun courses, links to Transcript and credentials. Build lean against the ladder2 learner record (theladder-shared/data-layer.js / the ai-academy/js/learner-record.js pattern in Aesop) — do NOT migrate Aesop's 1700-file ai-academy wholesale.

tasks.title: Rebuild the Transcript page
tasks.detail: Rebuild the durable learner transcript: completed / placed_out / verified / self_reported evidence, tier certifications, standards coverage, export + print. Source from theladder-shared/data-layer.js + the Firestore learner record.

tasks.title: Rebuild the Certifications support page
tasks.detail: Public-facing certification records / credential-meaning explanation. Support page per the 3-page vision (ladder2, transcript, student hub).

tasks.title: Finalize deploy.yml for theladderai
tasks.detail: The deploy workflow on main is a verbatim Aesop copy. Before enabling auto-deploy: (1) set server-dir to theladderai's FTP path — it is currently /public_html/aesop-academy/, which would deploy ONTO the live Aesop site; (2) remove the Aesop-specific 'Pre-deploy truncation guard' (.github/scripts/predeploy_check.py — not in this repo, so every run currently fails there before FTP) + the 'commit repairs back to main' step + the AESOP_PAT token; (3) confirm the FTP host/user (Mocahost 65.181.111.131 / hivetec1 — same or different for theladderai?). The server also needs secrets.php at the repo root (or an ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var) for aesop-api/proxy.php.

tasks.title: Prune / consolidate the migrated Ladder surface
tasks.detail: The import brought all of theladder/ (including the legacy /theladder/ index.html, authenticate.html, ladder-app.js) plus the products/use-cases admin pages. Decide what to keep vs. fold into the unified ladder2 experience (vision: collapse to 3 pages — ladder2, transcript, student hub).
